name: constellation
voice: a star chart drawn live — body words arrive as gold star-points that pop in at each letter, a hairline underline draws beneath, then the glyphs settle onto a faint declination/right-ascension grid; the apex word is handed off to the SKY, where stars twinkle in at its letter anchors, hairlines connect them in sequence, the word condenses in within the linework and a catalogue designation types beneath. Night quietly falls on the bright sky as it lands. Wonder, not spectacle.
when: science, astronomy, optics/imaging, research and discovery clips, contemplative explainers, anything about looking up or seeing further. Wants a clip that tolerates a soft dusk-dim (the sky goes night-blue behind the subject). Calm-to-elegant register.
